title: quercus phellodrys
intro: Quercus phellodrys, also known as the willow oak or swamp white oak, is a deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States. The leaves and bark of the Quercus phellodrys plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus phellodrys are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe skin irritations, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Quercus phellodrys is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, sore throats, and skin conditions.
